ddls:
CREATE TABLE "crime_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each crime report */,
  "district_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the district where the crime occurred */,
  "crime_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the crime type */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the crime occurred */,
  "hour_number" INTEGER /* Hour of the day when the crime occurred */,
  "report_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the crime incident */,
  "incident_location" TEXT /* Specific location where the crime occurred */,
  "reported_by" TEXT /* Name or ID of the person who reported the crime */,
  "report_status" TEXT /* Status of the crime report (e.g., open, closed, under investigation) */,
  "violent_crime_rate" REAL /* Rate of violent crimes per 100,000 people */,
  "assault_rate" REAL /* Rate of assaults per 100,000 people */,
  "robbery_rate" REAL /* Rate of robberies per 100,000 people */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crime_reports_district_id FOREIGN KEY ("district_id") REFERENCES districts ("district_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crime_reports_crime_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("crime_type_id") REFERENCES crime_types ("crime_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "districts" (
  "district_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each district */,
  "district_name" TEXT /* Name of the district */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the district */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the district */,
  "area" REAL /* Area of the district in square miles or kilometers */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("district_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "crime_types" (
  "crime_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each crime type */,
  "crime_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the crime type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the crime type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("crime_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "crime_rates" (
  "rate_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each crime rate */,
  "district_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the district */,
  "crime_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the crime type */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year for which the crime rate is calculated */,
  "month" INTEGER /* Month for which the crime rate is calculated */,
  "rate" REAL /* Crime rate per 100,000 people */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rate_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crime_rates_crime_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("crime_type_id") REFERENCES crime_types ("crime_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crime_rates_district_id FOREIGN KEY ("district_id") REFERENCES districts ("district_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed for security) */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., police officer, crime analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user (e.g., police, investigation, administration) */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the crime report being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the crime report */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the crime report was accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time when the crime report was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES crime_reports ("report_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "investigations" (
  "investigation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each investigation */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the crime report being investigated */,
  "investigator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the investigator leading the investigation */,
  "investigation_date" TEXT /* Date the investigation was launched */,
  "investigation_summary" TEXT /* Brief summary of the investigation */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the investigation (e.g., ongoing, closed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("investigation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_investigations_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES crime_reports ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_investigations_investigator_id FOREIGN KEY ("investigator_id") REFERENCES investigation_officers ("officer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "evidence" (
  "evidence_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each evidence */,
  "investigation_id" INTEGER /* ID of the investigation */,
  "evidence_type" TEXT /* Type of evidence (e.g., physical, witness statement) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the evidence */,
  "evidence_status" TEXT /* Status of the evidence (e.g., collected, analyzed, pending) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("evidence_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_evidence_investigation_id FOREIGN KEY ("investigation_id") REFERENCES investigations ("investigation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "offenders" (
  "offender_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each offender */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the crime report */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the offender */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the offender */,
  "offender_type" TEXT /* Type of offender (e.g., individual, organization) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("offender_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_offenders_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES crime_reports ("report_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "investigation_officers" (
  "officer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each investigation officer */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the investigation officer */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the investigation officer */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the investigation officer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("officer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "crime_report_updates" (
  "update_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each update */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the crime report being updated */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* Date of the update */,
  "updated_by" TEXT /* Name or ID of the person making the update */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("update_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crime_report_updates_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES crime_reports ("report_id")
)
